$comment: Phase 5.3 regulatory change-watch manifest. Seeded from baseline commit 217320f. --check is hermetic; --fetch runs weekly via .github/workflows/regulatory-watch.yml.

watchlist.notes: NIST maintains rev-5 catalog+baselines in the OSCAL-content repo; material changes arrive via new rev (next is Rev. 6). Patch errata occur a few times a year. Drift to 01f37cf9 was first observed 2026-05-18 and re-confirmed by direct curl the same day (upstream is now at NIST SP 800-53 rev5 v5.2.0, ~1196 controls). Deferred because adoption requires a content PR auditing every UC compliance.controlId mapping against the new catalog and regenerating data/crosswalks/oscal/nist-sp-800-53-r5.normalised.json. Note for next maintainer: src/splunk_uc/ingest/manifest.py:fetch() defaults to cache=True, so 'python -m splunk_uc ingest-oscal' will silently confirm the stale SHA unless vendor/oscal/nist_sp_800_53_r5_catalog.json is deleted first. See docs/regulatory-change-watch.md §4.2 for the force-fetch procedure.

openFinding.summary: SHA256 drift: was 1645df6a, now 01f37cf9 for nist-sp-800-53-r5 (deferred 2026-05-18; persistent, not transient)

watchlist.notes: CSF 2.0 is the current major; subsequent changes will be profile additions (e.g., quantum). Drift to 4836943f was first observed 2026-05-18 and re-confirmed by direct curl the same day (persistent). Deferred because adoption requires a content PR alongside the NIST 800-53 r5 adoption (the two ride together). See the cache pitfall note on the nist-800-53 entry for the force-fetch procedure.

openFinding.summary: SHA256 drift: was e3edb5ef, now 4836943f for nist-csf-v2 (deferred 2026-05-18; persistent, not transient)

watchlist.notes: Paywalled. Next refresh cycle is ~2027 per SC 27 roadmap. Track ISO news page and Annex A reorganisations via published amendments.
PCI SSC ships errata and minor versions on an irregular cadence (typically 1-2x per year). Bookmark the RSS-less Document Library. Grace dates are announced 6-12 months ahead.
EUR-Lex consolidated version. Original regulation text is stable; amendments arrive as separate instruments (e.g., the AI Act's data-protection-related recitals).
HHS/OCR publishes NPRMs and guidance via the HIPAA RSS. The December 2024 NPRM (HIPAA Security Rule update) is the active modernisation track.
Transposition deadline passed October 2024. Track Member-State implementing laws (ENISA tracker) plus Commission implementing acts.
Applied from 17 January 2025. RTS and ITS are the main change-delivery mechanism; track ESAs joint pages.
AICPA ASEC revises TSCs irregularly; most recent substantive revision was 2022. Watch the AICPA Technical Q&A and newsletter.
PCAOB issues staff guidance and proposed standards via their standards page. ITGC-relevant changes are rare but material (e.g., NOCLAR, AS 1000).
DoD CIO hosts the authoritative CMMC 2.0 page. 32 CFR Part 170 was finalised October 2024; DFARS 252.204-7021 rulemaking still in motion.

watchlist.regulationName: UK GDPR (Data Protection Act 2018 + UK GDPR)
watchlist.notes: Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 amends the UK GDPR; track legislation.gov.uk plus the ICO's legislative tracker for commencement orders.

openFinding.summary: ETag/Last-Modified drift: Mon, 20 Apr 2026 15:07:14 GMT -> Wed, 13 May 2026 15:07:58 GMT

watchlist.notes: ATT&CK ships two major releases per year (~April and October) plus point releases. SHA256 tracks the STIX 2.1 bundle. 17.1 drift to 987d0cfd was observed 2026-05-04 and adopted in v8.1.0. A subsequent drift to fc783039 was first observed 2026-05-18 and re-confirmed by direct curl the same day (persistent, not transient). Deferred because adoption requires a content PR that re-fetches all three ATT&CK domain bundles (enterprise + ics + mobile), regenerates the normalised crosswalks, and audits every UC sidecar's mitreAttack.techniqueIds against the new technique set for revoked/renamed IDs. See the cache pitfall note on the nist-800-53 entry for the force-fetch procedure (delete vendor/attack/*.json before re-running ingest-attack).

openFinding.summary: SHA256 drift: was 987d0cfd, now fc783039 for mitre-attack-enterprise (deferred 2026-05-18; persistent, not transient)

watchlist.notes: D3FEND releases are irregular; monitor ontology version in the JSON-LD header.
